issues.detail:
Ensure the contrast between foreground and background colors meets WCAG 2 AA minimum contrast ratio thresholds-Fix any of the following:
  Element has insufficient color contrast of 3.99 (foreground color: #71717b, background color: #0e0e11, font size: 10.5pt (14px), font weight: normal). Expected contrast ratio of 4.5:1

Ensure the contrast between foreground and background colors meets WCAG 2 AA minimum contrast ratio thresholds-Fix any of the following:
  Element has insufficient color contrast of 3.04 (foreground color: #ffffff, background color: #ff5f09, font size: 12.0pt (16px), font weight: bold). Expected contrast ratio of 4.5:1

Ensure the contrast between foreground and background colors meets WCAG 2 AA minimum contrast ratio thresholds-Fix any of the following:
  Element has insufficient color contrast of 3.73 (foreground color: #71717b, background color: #1b1516, font size: 9.0pt (12px), font weight: normal). Expected contrast ratio of 4.5:1

Ensure the order of headings is semantically correct-Fix any of the following:
  Heading order invalid

Ensure landmarks are unique-Fix any of the following:
  The landmark must have a unique aria-label, aria-labelledby, or title to make landmarks distinguishable

Largest Contentful Paint marks the time at which the largest text or image is painted. [Learn more about the Largest Contentful Paint metric](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/lighthouse/performance/lighthouse-largest-contentful-paint/)

suggestions.suggestion: The text color is too light against the dark background, making it hard for users to read. Update the text color to a lighter shade (e.g., #a1a1aa) to meet the 4.5:1 contrast requirement.

The high transparency of this button makes the text difficult to read against the background color. Adjust the opacity or choose a darker text color to ensure the contrast ratio meets the 4.5:1 accessibility standard.

This text does not have enough contrast against its background, which violates accessibility guidelines for readability. Use a brighter grey or white color to improve legibility for users with visual impairments.

The page skips heading levels (e.g., jumping from an h2 to an h4), which confuses screen readers. Ensure headings follow a logical, nested structure (h1 -> h2 -> h3) to help users navigate the content hierarchy.

Multiple navigation landmarks are present without distinct labels, making it impossible for screen reader users to tell them apart. Add a unique 'aria-label' attribute to each <nav> element to clarify their specific purpose.

The main content is taking too long to load, which negatively impacts user experience and SEO rankings. Optimize the LCP by compressing large images, using modern formats like WebP, or preloading the primary hero image.
